name: Pinwheel Pumpkin Cookies
recipeInstructions:
- For dough:
- 4 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup shortening
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 3 eggs
- For filling:
- In a medium saucepan, combine pumpkin, sugar, and pumpkin pie spice. Bring to a boil.
- Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
- Stir in chopped nuts and let cool.
- To assemble:
- Divide dough into three portions.
- On floured foil, roll out one portion of dough into an 8 x 12-inch rectangle, keeping the remaining dough chilled.
- Spread with about a third of the filling. Starting from the wide end, roll up as for a jellyroll.
- Wrap in foil and repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
- Freeze rolls several hours or overnight.
- Remove one roll at a time from the freezer; unwrap and cut into 3/8-inch slices.
- Place on a greased cookie sheet. Bake in a preheated 400-degree oven for 10 to 12 minutes, or until golden.
- Cool on a wire rack.
- For icing: In a medium bowl, combine powdered sugar, butter, vanilla, and milk; blend until smooth.
